channel.title: math.GM updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.GM updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Deficiency Identity for Zeta Values and Optimal Approximation
item.description: arXiv:2604.16530v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce a deficiency-based representation and approximation framework for odd values of the Riemann zeta function. The method is built upon a nonlinear transformation of partial sums of $\zeta(p)$, yielding an exact decomposition \[ \zeta(q) = \zeta(p)^{q/p} - D_\infty^{(p,q)}, \] where $D_\infty^{(p,q)}$ is a cumulative deficiency functional. We derive a corrected estimator that removes first-order bias and establish asymptotic convergence rates of the form \[ B_n^{(p,q)} - \zeta(q) = O\!\left(n^{-\min(2p-2,\;q-1)}\right). \] In particular, for odd $q=2m+1$, the optimized choice $p=2m=q-1$ yields \[ B_n^{(q-1,q)} - \zeta(q) = O(n^{-(q-1)}), \] recovering high-order convergence without explicit Euler--Maclaurin expansions. We further extend the framework to general spectral zeta functions associated with eigenvalue sequences $\{\lambda_k\}$, showing that convergence behavior depends on the interplay between the base exponent and spectral growth rate. Numerical experiments confirm the theoretical predictions, demonstrate substantial improvements over non-optimized variants, and illustrate the adaptability of the method across classical and spectral settings.
item.dc:creator: Meisam Mohammady

item.title: Sine laws on semigroups with an involutive anti-automorphism: A Levi--Civita approach via left translations
item.description: arXiv:2511.00019v5 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Stetk\ae r's matrix (Levi--Civita) method is a powerful tool for functional equations on semigroups involving a homomorphism $\sigma$, as it yields a finite-dimensional invariant space under right translations and a corresponding matrix formalism. However, this framework collapses when $\sigma$ is an involutive anti-automorphism due to the order reversal in the right-regular action. In this paper, we overcome this obstruction at the operator level by establishing the conjugation identity: letting $J$ denote composition with $\sigma$, we prove \[ J\,R(\sigma(y))\,J=L(y)\qquad(\forall\,y\in S), \] which converts the problematic right translates into left translations. Using this left-translation approach, we obtain an anti-automorphic Levi--Civita closure principle and apply it to the generalized sine law. Remarkably, the classical dichotomy $\beta\in\{\pm1\}$ and the parity relation $f\circ\sigma=\beta f$ are recovered unconditionally. Furthermore, under a natural bridge hypothesis, which is automatically satisfied when there exists a central element $c$ with $f(c)\neq 0$, we obtain the corresponding standard $xy$-addition law and the exact $\sigma$-transformation rule for $g$.
item.dc:creator: Dang Vo Phuc
